summary: Persisted print identity must prefer task_id, preserve payload ownership across stale subtask reuse, and keep printer-scoped history migration lossless.
reason: Bambu can retain a prior subtask_id and remaining-time estimate when a new job starts. Keying by that transient value split one print, contaminated it with the preceding job, and duplicated legacy unscoped history.
validated_outcome: The live database migrated with integrity ok: the Benchy has one task-scoped run and exactly its own start/finish transitions, the preceding print remains separate, and no unscoped jobs remain.
